diff_user.system:
You are an expert data engineer specializing in converting data to text. You will be given the current Python program with its BLUE score and an inspiration program and your task will be to evolve the current Python program to improve the score. It is a program that converts a list of triples into natural language text. The given triples will be associated with the topic: Food. The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence. Converting all those triples into one sentence will award greater score. An example of a complex sentence:
Triples:
(Antwerp | cityServed | Antwerp International Airport)
(Belgium | country | Antwerp)
(City of Brussels | capital | Belgium)
Example sentence:
"Antwerp International Airport serves the city of Antwerp which is in Belgium, where the capital is Brussels."

The 'predict' function returns that sentence as a string. Below is the list of all possible predicates that can be given as an input. With the given predicates and example triples implement the 'predict' function so it can process all the possible predicates:

Predicate: country - Example triple: (Ajoblanco | country | Spain)
Predicate: ingredient - Example triple: (Ajoblanco | ingredient | Almond)
Predicate: region - Example triple: (Ajoblanco | region | Andalusia)
Predicate: division - Example triple: (Almond | division | Flowering plant)
Predicate: family - Example triple: (Almond | family | Rosaceae)
Predicate: order - Example triple: (Almond | order | Rosales)
Predicate: course - Example triple: (Amatriciana sauce | course | Italian meal structure)
Predicate: dishVariation - Example triple: (Amatriciana sauce | dishVariation | "Onion, garlic, black pepper, chili")
Predicate: mainIngredient - Example triple: (Amatriciana sauce | mainIngredient | "Tomatoes, guanciale, cheese, olive oil")
Predicate: leader - Example triple: (Andalusia | leader | Susana Díaz)
Predicate: alternativeName - Example triple: (Asam pedas | alternativeName | "Asam padeh")
Predicate: creatorOfDish - Example triple: (Asam pedas | creatorOfDish | Malay cuisine)
Predicate: creator - Example triple: (Bakewell pudding | creator | "Rutland Arms, Bakewell, in 1820")
Predicate: servingTemperature - Example triple: (Bakewell pudding | servingTemperature | "Warm (freshly baked) or cold")
Predicate: carbohydrate - Example triple: (Barny Cakes | carbohydrate | 18.0 g)
Predicate: fat - Example triple: (Barny Cakes | fat | 4.8 g)
Predicate: year - Example triple: (Barny Cakes | year | 1999)
Predicate: related - Example triple: (Bhajji | related | Pakora)
Predicate: similarDish - Example triple: (Bhajji | similarDish | "Pakora and other fritters made from wheat or corn flour")
Predicate: genus - Example triple: (Celery | genus | Apium)
Predicate: language - Example triple: (China | language | Standard Chinese)
Predicate: isPartOf - Example triple: (Derbyshire Dales | isPartOf | Derbyshire)
Predicate: currency - Example triple: (France | currency | Euro)
Predicate: demonym - Example triple: (India | demonym | Indian people)
Predicate: capital - Example triple: (Indonesia | capital | Jakarta)
Predicate: ethnicGroup - Example triple: (Java | ethnicGroup | Javanese people)
Predicate: foundationPlace - Example triple: (Mondelez International | foundationPlace | Chicago)

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of food‑related triples into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Group triples by subject for better sentence construction
    subject_info = {}
    for t in triples:
        s, p, o = t.subject, t.predicate, t.object
        if s not in subject_info:
            subject_info[s] = []
        subject_info[s].append((p, o))

    # Build connected sentences using relative clauses
    sentences = []
    processed_subjects = set()

    for subject in subject_info:
        if subject in processed_subjects:
            continue

        info = subject_info[subject]
        base_sentence = None
        relative_clauses = []

        for p, o in info:
            if p == "country":
                relative_clauses.append(f"which is from {o}")
            elif p == "ingredient":
                relative_clauses.append(f"containing {o}")
            elif p == "region":
                relative_clauses.append(f"originating from {o}")
            elif p == "division":
                relative_clauses.append(f"belonging to the division {o}")
            elif p == "family":
                relative_clauses.append(f"in the family {o}")
            elif p == "order":
                relative_clauses.append(f"classified under the order {o}")
            elif p == "course":
                relative_clauses.append(f"served as a {o}")
            elif p == "dishVariation":
                relative_clauses.append(f"with variations such as {o}")
            elif p == "mainIngredient":
                relative_clauses.append(f"whose main ingredients are {o}")
            elif p == "leader":
                relative_clauses.append(f"led by {o}")
            elif p == "alternativeName":
                relative_clauses.append(f"also known as {o}")
            elif p == "creatorOfDish" or p == "creator":
                relative_clauses.append(f"created by {o}")
            elif p == "servingTemperature" or p == "served":
                relative_clauses.append(f"served {o}")
            elif p == "carbohydrate":
                relative_clauses.append(f"containing {o} of carbohydrates")
            elif p == "fat":
                relative_clauses.append(f"containing {o} of fat")
            elif p == "protein":
                relative_clauses.append(f"containing {o} of protein")
            elif p == "year":
                relative_clauses.append(f"introduced in {o}")
            elif p == "related":
                relative_clauses.append(f"related to {o}")
            elif p == "similarDish":
                relative_clauses.append(f"similar to {o}")
            elif p == "genus":
                relative_clauses.append(f"of the genus {o}")
            elif p == "language":
                relative_clauses.append(f"with language {o}")
            elif p == "isPartOf":
                relative_clauses.append(f"part of {o}")
            elif p == "currency":
                relative_clauses.append(f"with currency {o}")
            elif p == "demonym":
                relative_clauses.append(f"with demonym {o}")
            elif p == "capital":
                relative_clauses.append(f"with capital {o}")
            elif p == "ethnicGroup":
                relative_clauses.append(f"with ethnic group {o}")
            elif p == "foundationPlace":
                relative_clauses.append(f"founded in {o}")
            elif p == "officialLanguage":
                relative_clauses.append(f"with official language {o}")
            elif p == "leaderTitle":
                relative_clauses.append(f"with leader title {o}")
            elif p == "servingSize":
                relative_clauses.append(f"with serving size {o}")

        if relative_clauses:
            if len(relative_clauses) == 1:
                base_sentence = f"{subject} {relative_clauses[0]}"
            else:
                base_sentence = f"{subject}, {', '.join(relative_clauses[:-1])}, and {relative_clauses[-1]}"
        else:
            base_sentence = subject

        sentences.append(base_sentence)
        processed_subjects.add(subject)

    # Combine all sentences into one coherent sentence
    if len(sentences) == 1:
        sentence = sentences[0] + "."
    elif len(sentences) == 2:
        sentence = f"{sentences[0]}, and {sentences[1]}."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(sentences[:-1]) + f", and {sentences[-1]}."
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of food‑related triples into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Build graph of relationships
    graph = {}
    for t in triples:
        s, p, o = t.subject, t.predicate, t.object
        if s not in graph:
            graph[s] = []
        graph[s].append((p, o))
    
    # Find the main subject (food item - typically has most food-related predicates)
    food_predicates = {"ingredient", "mainIngredient", "dishVariation", "course", 
                       "servingTemperature", "served", "carbohydrate", "fat", "protein",
                       "year", "related", "similarDish", "alternativeName", "creator", 
                       "creatorOfDish", "servingSize"}
    
    main_subject = None
    max_food_preds = 0
    
    for subject in graph:
        food_count = sum(1 for p, o in graph[subject] if p in food_predicates)
        if food_count > max_food_preds:
            max_food_preds = food_count
            main_subject = subject
    
    if not main_subject:
        main_subject = list(graph.keys())[0]
    
    # Build connected sentence chain
    visited = set()
    parts = []
    
    def get_predicate_phrase(p, o):
        phrases = {
            "country": f"from {o}",
            "region": f"originating in {o}",
            "ingredient": f"containing {o}",
            "mainIngredient": f"with main ingredients {o}",
            "dishVariation": f"variations include {o}",
            "course": f"as a {o}",
            "servingTemperature": f"served {o}",
            "served": f"served {o}",
            "carbohydrate": f"with {o} carbohydrates",
            "fat": f"with {o} fat",
            "protein": f"with {o} protein",
            "year": f"introduced in {o}",
            "related": f"related to {o}",
            "similarDish": f"similar to {o}",
            "alternativeName": f"also known as {o}",
            "creator": f"created by {o}",
            "creatorOfDish": f"created by {o}",
            "servingSize": f"with serving size {o}",
            "division": f"division {o}",
            "family": f"family {o}",
            "order": f"order {o}",
            "genus": f"genus {o}",
            "capital": f"capital {o}",
            "isPartOf": f"part of {o}",
            "foundationPlace": f"found in {o}",
            "leader": f"led by {o}",
            "language": f"language {o}",
            "officialLanguage": f"official language {o}",
            "currency": f"currency {o}",
            "demonym": f"demonym {o}",
            "ethnicGroup": f"ethnic group {o}",
            "leaderTitle": f"leader title {o}",
            "class": f"class {o}"
        }
        return phrases.get(p, f"{p} {o}")
    
    # Process main subject and its direct relationships
    if main_subject in graph:
        clauses = []
        for p, o in graph[main_subject]:
            if p in ["country", "region", "isPartOf"]:
                clauses.append(f"which is {get_predicate_phrase(p, o)}")
            else:
                clauses.append(f"{get_predicate_phrase(p, o)}")
        
        if clauses:
            if len(clauses) == 1:
                parts.append(f"{main_subject} {clauses[0]}")
            else:
                all_but_last = ", ".join(clauses[:-1])
                parts.append(f"{main_subject} {all_but_last}, and {clauses[-1]}")
    
    # Handle connected subjects (like country -> capital)
    for subject, relations in graph.items():
        if subject == main_subject or subject in visited:
            continue
        
        for p, o in relations:
            if subject in parts[0] if parts else False:
                continue
            if p in ["capital", "leader", "currency", "language", "officialLanguage", "demonym", "ethnicGroup"]:
                for prev_part in parts:
                    if o in prev_part:
                        parts.append(f"where the {p} is {subject}")
                        break
    
    if not parts:
        return main_subject + "."
    
    sentence = parts[0]
    for i, part in enumerate(parts[1:], 1):
        if i == 1:
            sentence += f", and {part}"
        else:
            sentence += f", {part}"
    
    return sentence + "."
